steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a parenthetical phrase interrupting a relative clause on the left, and a passive infinitive on the right that needs a modal verb. The blank must close the parenthetical with a comma and supply the missing modal 'could' before 'be easily carved.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — malleability-could: Does not include the closing comma for the parenthetical, leaving the interruption improperly set off. ✗
  • Choice B — malleability, could: Closes the parenthetical with a comma and supplies the required modal verb, making the sentence grammatically complete. ✓
  • Choice C — malleability could: Uses unconventional punctuation (a dash or semicolon) instead of a closing comma, breaking the parenthetical structure. ✗
  • Choice D — malleability could-: Omits 'could,' leaving the passive infinitive without a modal and breaking the verb structure. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
